items.html: India generates over 62 million tonnes of solid waste annually — yet most of it could be prevented with basic civic habit changes like composting and segregation.
Article 51A of the Indian Constitution lists "to protect and improve the natural environment" as a Fundamental Duty of every citizen. Littering technically violates the Constitution!
Honking adds to noise pollution which WHO links to hearing loss, sleep disorders, and cardiovascular disease. India's cities rank among the noisiest in the world.
The first Right to Information (RTI) case in India was filed by an ordinary farmer in Rajasthan — proving every citizen has real power against the system.
Paying taxes on time is a civic duty — but only 1.5% of Indians file income tax returns, meaning the burden falls on a tiny fraction of citizens.
